title: Queue Settings
lede: When an agent transfers a call to this group as a queue, the customer is held while the group's online members are rung until one answers. Set the maximum wait, what happens on timeout, and the on-hold audio.

steps.title: Open the Queue Settings tab
steps.content:
1. Go to Call Center → Contact Center → Groups and select a group.
  2. Open the Queue Settings tab.

images.caption: Queue Settings: max wait, timeout action, and queue hold audio.
Queue Settings: max wait, timeout action, and queue hold audio.

steps.title: Set the maximum wait
steps.content: In Max wait in queue (seconds), enter how long a queued customer waits before the timeout action fires. Enter 0 to use the system default (30 minutes).

steps.title: Choose the on-timeout action
steps.content:
Under When the max wait is reached, choose what happens if nobody answers in time:

  • Hang up the call.
  • Transfer to an AI assistant — then pick the assistant that takes the call.

This is the safety net so a waiting customer is never stranded.

steps.title: Set the queue hold audio
steps.content: Toggle Play hold music while queued to play your configured hold or wait music while the caller is in this queue. Optionally add Queue periodic messages — spoken lines that loop while the caller is held. You can use tokens like {first_name} and {callers_queue_position}, which updates live with the caller's place in line. Click Save.

metaDescription: Configure Contact Center queue settings in iMash: max wait time, on-timeout action (hang up or hand to AI), queue size, and on-hold audio.
metaKeywords: queue settings, max wait, timeout, voicemail, ai fallback, hold music, contact center, permission group
